description: A list endpoint with no pagination contract returns the entire collection, degrading as data grows and offering no stable iteration.
body: Failure mode: a collection endpoint returns all items because paging was never designed, working in testing but failing at scale. Detection signals: response sizes and latencies grow with the dataset; clients time out on large collections; there is no documented way to page. Do not ship a growable collection without a paging contract. Safer fallback: define cursor-based paging with a page size and next-page mechanism before launch, keep ordering stable, and verify a client can iterate all pages as data changes.
